options.label: All lights — none of them work
One or more lights don't work (others are fine)

te-q2.text: Are the light switches in the towing vehicle turned on?

te-a1.text: Turn on the light switches in the towing vehicle
te-a1.actionText: Turn on the towing vehicle's light switches. Check that headlights, taillights, and running lights are activated. Recheck trailer lights.

te-q3.text: Check the intervehicular cable connector between the trailer and towing vehicle. Is it fully seated?

options.label: No — connector is loose or partially pulled out

te-a2.actionText: Pull the cable connector fully out, inspect the pins, then firmly push it back in until it locks. Recheck trailer lights.

te-q4.text: Inspect the connector pins on both the trailer and towing vehicle ends. Are the pins dirty or corroded (green/white buildup)?

options.label: Yes — pins are dirty or corroded

te-a3.actionText: Use a dry rag or contact cleaner to clean the connector pins on both ends. Remove all dirt, corrosion, and moisture. Reseat the connector firmly and recheck lights.

te-q4c.text: Do trailer lights work after cleaning the contacts?

te-q4b.text: Inspect the cable and wiring harness along the trailer frame. Are there any visibly broken, cut, or pinched wires?

te-q5.text: Inspect the connector pins at the trailer-to-towing-vehicle cable. Are the pins dirty or corroded?

te-a4.actionText: Use a dry rag or contact cleaner to clean the connector pins. Remove all dirt, corrosion, and moisture. Reseat the connector firmly and recheck the affected lights.

te-q5b.text: Do the affected lights work after cleaning?

te-q6.text: Inspect the wiring at the base of each affected light. Are the wires loose or broken where they connect to the light?

options.label: Yes — loose or broken wires found

te-q8.text: Inspect the connector pins at the trailer-to-towing-vehicle cable. Are the pins dirty or corroded?

te-a5.actionText: Use a dry rag or contact cleaner to clean the connector pins. Remove all dirt, corrosion, and moisture. Reseat the connector firmly and recheck light brightness.

te-q8b.text: Are lights now at normal brightness (steady, not flickering)?

te-q9.text: Inspect the wiring at the base of the affected lights. Are any wires loose where they connect to the light?

terminal.summary: All trailer lights restored — switch or connector reseated

te-t2.text: Cable or wiring harness damage found. Notify maintenance — trailer lights cannot be fixed at operator level.

terminal.summary: Trailer wiring harness damage — all lights inoperative, needs maintenance

te-t3.text: Affected lights restored after cleaning connector contacts.

terminal.summary: Partial trailer light failure — corroded connector contacts cleaned, lights restored

te-t4.text: Loose or broken wires at affected light. Notify maintenance.

terminal.summary: Loose/broken wires at affected trailer light — needs maintenance

te-t5.text: Dim/flickering lights resolved after cleaning connector contacts.

terminal.summary: Dim/flickering trailer lights — corroded contacts cleaned, lights normal

te-t6.text: Loose wires at light fixture or no obvious cause found. Notify maintenance.

terminal.summary: Dim/flickering trailer lights — loose wiring, needs maintenance

options.label: Brakes will not release — trailer dragging
Brakes will not hold when engaged

tb-q2.text: Check both handbrake levers on the trailer. Are they fully released (in the down/off position)?

options.label: No — one or both levers were still engaged
Yes — both levers are fully released

tb-a1.actionText: Push both handbrake levers fully down to the released position. Confirm they are seated in the release detent. Attempt to roll the trailer by hand to confirm brakes are free.

tb-q2b.text: Are the brakes now released and trailer rolling freely?

tb-q3.text: Check the breakaway lever (emergency brake). Is it engaged or pulled out?

options.label: No — breakaway lever is in the normal position

tb-a2.actionText: Return the breakaway lever to its normal (disengaged) position. This lever is typically a pull pin or cable — reset it per the label on the trailer. Confirm brakes are now free.

tb-q3b.text: Are the brakes now released and trailer rolling freely?

tb-q5.text: Check both handbrake levers. Are they fully engaged (in the up/on position)?

options.label: No — levers were not fully up

tb-a3.actionText: Pull both handbrake levers fully up to the engaged position. Confirm they click into the lock detent. Attempt to roll the trailer to confirm brakes are holding.

tb-q5b.text: Are brakes now holding with the trailer stationary?

tb-q6.text: Check the brake fluid reservoir. Is the fluid level below the MIN mark?

options.label: Yes — fluid is low or empty

tb-a4.text: Add brake fluid to the reservoir
tb-a4.actionText: Add the correct brake fluid (check the reservoir cap for type) to bring the level up to the MAX mark. Do not overfill. Clean up any spills — brake fluid damages paint.

tb-q6b.text: Do the hydraulic brakes work now after adding fluid?

tb-q7.text: Inspect the brake tubes and hoses along the trailer frame. Do you see any wet spots, drips, or signs of a brake fluid leak?

tb-q9.text: Inspect the handbrake lever. Is it seized (won't move at all) or visibly damaged?

options.label: Seized — won't move but looks intact
Damaged — bent, broken, or missing parts

tb-a5.text: Clean and lubricate the handbrake lever
tb-a5.actionText: Clean the lever pivot points with a rag to remove dirt and rust. Apply a light coat of lubricant (WD-40 or equivalent) to the pivot and cable attachment points. Work the lever back and forth several times.

tb-q9b.text: Does the handbrake lever move freely now?

tb-t1.text: Brakes released. Handbrake lever or breakaway lever was the cause.

terminal.summary: Brakes not releasing — handbrake or breakaway lever repositioned, brakes now free

tb-t2.text: Brakes still will not release after checking both levers. Notify maintenance — do not tow the trailer.

terminal.summary: Brakes locked — beyond operator repair, NMC safety-critical

tb-t3.text: Handbrake levers are fully engaged but brakes still will not hold. Notify maintenance — do not park on any slope.

terminal.summary: Brakes not holding — beyond operator repair, NMC safety-critical

tb-t4.text: Brakes now holding. Levers were not fully engaged.

terminal.summary: Brakes not holding — handbrake levers fully engaged, brakes now holding

tb-t5.text: Hydraulic brakes restored after adding brake fluid. Monitor for further fluid loss — low fluid indicates a possible leak that needs follow-up maintenance.

terminal.summary: Hydraulic brakes restored — brake fluid topped off, monitor for leak

tb-t6.text: Brake fluid leak found, or hydraulic brakes still inoperative. This is a safety-critical NO-GO. Do not tow. Notify maintenance immediately.

terminal.summary: Hydraulic brake failure — brake fluid leak or unresolved, NMC safety-critical

tb-t7.text: Handbrake lever is damaged or cannot be fixed at operator level. Notify maintenance.

terminal.summary: Handbrake lever inoperative — damage or seized beyond operator repair

tb-t8.text: Handbrake lever is now operational after cleaning and lubrication.

terminal.summary: Handbrake lever seized — cleaned and lubricated, lever now operational

ttd-q2.text: Check the tire pressure. Is it below 17 PSI (±2 PSI)?

ttd-a1.actionText: Inflate the tire to 17 PSI (±2 PSI). Use a tire pressure gauge to confirm. Uneven wear caused by low pressure may require maintenance inspection of the tire and alignment.

ttd-q2b.text: Is the tire now at 15–19 PSI and holding pressure?

ttd-q4.text: Inspect the wheel stud nuts (lug nuts). Are any missing or visibly loose?

options.label: Yes — missing or loose studs/lug nuts found

ttd-a2.text: Replace missing or tighten loose stud nuts
ttd-a2.actionText: Replace any missing stud nuts from BII if available. Hand-tighten all loose lug nuts in a star pattern. NOTE: Proper torque (85–115 ft-lbs) requires a torque wrench — notify maintenance to verify torque after this field fix.

ttd-q4b.text: After tightening, does the wheel still wobble?

ttd-t1.text: Tire pressure is correct but uneven wear is present. Notify maintenance to inspect the tire and wheel alignment.

terminal.summary: Uneven tire wear with correct pressure — possible alignment or tire damage, needs maintenance

ttd-t2.text: Tire inflated to correct pressure. Monitor wear on next PMCS. Notify maintenance if uneven wear continues.

terminal.summary: Uneven tire wear — low pressure corrected, inflated to 17 PSI

ttd-t3.text: Tire will not hold pressure. Possible puncture or bead damage. Notify maintenance.

terminal.summary: Trailer tire will not hold pressure — needs replacement

ttd-t4.text: Wobbly wheel with no loose lug nuts, or wheel still wobbling after tightening. Possible bearing or hub damage. Do not tow — notify maintenance.

terminal.summary: Wobbly wheel — possible bearing or hub damage, NMC

ttd-t5.text: Wheel secured after tightening lug nuts. Notify maintenance to verify proper torque (85–115 ft-lbs) before next mission.

terminal.summary: Wobbly wheel — loose lug nuts tightened, maintenance needed to verify torque

tsl-t1.text: Front support leg will not crank. Inspect for dents or physical damage to the leg or crank mechanism. This is beyond operator repair — notify maintenance.

terminal.summary: Front support leg inoperative — dents or damage found, needs maintenance

ts-q1.text: The trailer is leaning to one side. Check the tire pressure on both tires. Is either tire below 17 PSI?

options.label: Yes — one or both tires are low
No — both tires are at correct pressure

ts-a1.actionText: Inflate both tires to 17 PSI. Use a tire pressure gauge to confirm both are equal. A significant difference in pressure between sides can cause the trailer to lean.

ts-q1b.text: Is the trailer now level after inflating the tires?

ts-q2.text: Inspect the shock absorbers on both sides. Do you see any wet spots or fluid leaking from either shock?

ts-q3.text: Compare the shock absorber extension rods on both sides. Are they visibly uneven — one side extended significantly more than the other?

ts-t1.text: Trailer is now level after correcting tire pressure.

terminal.summary: Trailer leaning — uneven tire pressure corrected, trailer now level

ts-t2.text: Shock absorber leak, uneven extension, or trailer still leaning with correct tire pressure. Notify maintenance.

terminal.summary: Trailer leaning — shock absorber failure or suspension issue, needs maintenance
